description_long: This object is a classic CRT computer monitor with a boxy, deep profile and a slightly curved display screen. The front features a thick, matte bezel framing the screen, with three small, round buttons located at the bottom right of the bezel. The sides and rear of the monitor are smoothly contoured, bulging outward to accommodate the cathode ray tube, and the back is flat with no visible ports or ventilation grilles. The monitor is primarily dark gray to black in color, with a slightly lighter bezel on the front. The surface appears hard and matte, typical of older plastic electronics. There are no visible logos, stands, or branding elements on the exterior.
description: A bulky, dark gray CRT computer monitor with a thick matte bezel and three buttons on the front. The sides and back are smoothly contoured and solid, with no visible ports or logos.
description_view_0: Front view showing the thick bezel, large display area, and three small buttons at the bottom right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep, curved back and the solid, seamless plastic casing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, featureless back panel with no visible ports or branding.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the monitor's depth and smooth, curved rear profile.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and matte, with squared corners and a slightly lighter gray color than the rest of the monitor. It has a simple, utilitarian appearance with no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: No stand is visible; the monitor appears to rest directly on its flat base.
attribute_button_placement: Three small, round buttons are grouped together at the bottom right of the front bezel.
